mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and operation of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators managing clusters across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API components
steps.description:
This command installs the Cluster API components into the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace cluster-api-system

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace cluster-api-system --version v1.12.4

```

> Create a namespace for Cluster API.
> Install Cluster API with the specified version.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace cluster-api-system --version v1.12.4

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Provider
steps.description:
This step installs the provider for your infrastructure (e.g., AWS, Azure). Replace 'aws' with your desired provider.

```bash

helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace cluster-api-system --version v1.12.4

```

> Install the AWS provider for Cluster API.
commands: helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace cluster-api-system --version v1.12.4

steps.title: Create a Cluster Resource
steps.description:
This command creates a new Cluster resource, which defines the desired state of your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster
EOF

```

> Replace 'my-cluster' with your desired cluster name.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster Resource
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Cluster resource to ensure it is ready.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -A

```

> Verify that the cluster is created and check its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and may result in data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace cluster-api-system

kubectl delete namespace cluster-api-system

```

> Uninstall the Cluster API components.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your cluster resources and configurations. This process includes draining nodes if necessary.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -A -o yaml > cluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace cluster-api-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl get clusters -A

```

> Backup current cluster resources.
> Upgrade Cluster API to the specified version.
> Verify that the clusters are in a healthy state post-upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace cluster-api-system --version v1.12.4

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: unable to create infrastructure'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfigured provider credentials or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Check your provider credentials and ensure they have the necessary permissions to create resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may be in a pending state due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the events associated with the cluster using 'kubectl describe cluster <cluster-name>' to identify issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig context may not be set correctly or the API server is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is set up correctly and that the API server is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to find the specified Helm chart'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Helm repository is not added or updated.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo update' to refresh the repository list.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API versions for Cluster and Machine resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your resource definitions to the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters -A' showing your cluster in a 'Ready' state.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace cluster-api-system --version v1.12.4
helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace cluster-api-system --version v1.12.4

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and Helm installed.
